name: Thomas Ford
title: Senior Backend Engineer
summary: Senior Backend Engineer with 8+ years building scalable, highly available Python and Node.js services on AWS for healthcare and consumer-facing platforms. Designs maintainable, well-tested APIs and event-driven architectures while owning full delivery cycles from design through production monitoring. Reduced patient-service processing time by 24% and stabilized critical care journeys at 99.94% availability by applying sound architectural patterns, disciplined database optimization, and close cross-functional alignment with product, science, and operations stakeholders.

experience.title: Senior Backend Engineer
responsibilities: Architected Python and Node.js FastAPI services across 12 patient-facing workflows — including scheduling, virtual care, reminders, and payments — owning full delivery from design through production monitoring with CloudWatch and Datadog on AWS.

Directed end-to-end technical ownership of 7 CVS Virtual Care and MinuteClinic® backend feature areas, translating regulatory and privacy requirements into HIPAA-aligned, RBAC-enforced APIs deployed across containerized AWS environments serving roughly 4 million monthly active users.

Orchestrated architectural improvements across 4 engineering workstreams using Docker, Kubernetes, and GitHub Actions CI/CD, establishing maintainability standards and documented OpenAPI contracts that reduced onboarding ramp for 3 new engineers by about 40%.
achievements: Increased appointment-flow completion by 31% by redesigning Python eligibility and registration service logic, eliminating ambiguous state transitions and instrumenting abandonment points with structured CloudWatch metrics.

Reduced patient-service processing time by 24% by unifying scheduling, prescription, and benefits data behind optimized PostgreSQL schemas and Redis caching layers, cutting median API latency to sub-200ms.
Stabilized high-traffic care journeys at 99.94% uptime by re-architecting failure-handling, health-check, and circuit-breaker patterns across containerized Python microservices on AWS ECS.
Automated same-day reminder and cancellation pipelines through event-driven asynchronous workers, lowering manual outreach volume by 18% for MinuteClinic® operations teams without adding headcount.
Introduced 6 LLM-assisted tooling prototypes for test generation, support summarization, and workflow analysis, cutting selected investigation and triage cycles by roughly 30%.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
responsibilities: Led backend architecture for 9 patient-intake and care-coordination services using Python, C#/.NET, and REST APIs, designing scalable database schemas across SQL Server that handled 11 integrated data sources for clinical and billing stakeholders.

Unified 5 legacy EHR, document, billing, and notification integrations behind versioned, Swagger-documented service contracts, enabling product and operations teams to ship intake changes through a single auditable workflow with full HIPAA-aligned audit logging.

Established release ownership for 3 production backend applications through Jenkins CI/CD pipelines, role-based access controls, and quarterly security review cycles conducted with compliance, clinical, and engineering leadership.
achievements: Streamlined referral validation and task-routing logic to cut intake handoff time in half, preserving nurse review checkpoints and all PHI-handling controls required under HIPAA regulatory standards.

Reduced peak SQL Server response time by 38% through query restructuring, composite index tuning, and background-job rescheduling across care-coordination dashboard endpoints serving 200+ daily clinical users.
Eliminated 42% of repeat production defects by expanding xUnit, Postman integration, and Cypress end-to-end coverage across admission, document-processing, and provider-communication service paths.
Modernized 14 backend endpoints and their corresponding React screens, improving first-pass intake completion by 27% across clinical and administrative user cohorts.

Resolved one-third of admission-status escalations by exposing cleaner workflow-state APIs and operational alert hooks to coordinators and product owners, reducing mean time to resolution by about 35%.

experience.title: Software Engineer
responsibilities: Designed and deployed 8 MyTeamCare patient-portal backend capabilities — covering online scheduling, paperless registration, provider messaging, and medical-record access — using Python, JavaScript, REST APIs, and PostgreSQL with acceptance criteria co-authored by physicians, front-desk staff, and infrastructure teams.

Managed integration scope for 16 EHR and patient-management data mappings, building schema validation and transformation layers that standardized data contracts across 3 upstream clinical systems for 2 portal release trains.

Standardized backend delivery through Git-based review workflows, automated API documentation, Linux deployment runbooks, and integration test suites that covered roughly 80% of critical patient-data endpoints.
achievements: Transformed appointment booking into a single-session guided backend flow, raising successful self-service scheduling by 33% without requiring additional call-center staffing.

Accelerated provider-directory search APIs to sub-second median response time by restructuring SQL query plans, applying targeted index changes, and introducing query-result caching for high-frequency lookups.
Implemented secure session, authorization, and server-side input-validation controls that reduced account-access incidents by approximately 20% across patient and staff user segments.
Simplified paperless registration service logic to cut incomplete form submissions by 22%, improving data completeness for downstream billing and clinical record systems.

Revitalized 11 legacy portal backend modules, decreasing release-related regressions by 29% by introducing reusable service components and expanding integration-test coverage across data-sensitive patient workflows.

experience.title: Junior Software Engineer
responsibilities: Developed 10 scheduling and field-operations backend modules using JavaScript, C#/.NET, and MySQL, delivering validated appointment, customer, and service-record workflows across 3 internal product areas with direct input from dispatchers and operations leaders.

Controlled data integrity across 15 server-side validation and permission rules, co-defining acceptance criteria with operations stakeholders to ensure consistent enforcement across customer, appointment, and service-record data models.

Initiated a biweekly backend release cadence through Git-based code reviews, unit-test suites, and structured deployment checklists that reduced unplanned rollbacks by about 25% over the role's final year.
achievements: Launched automated email notification services across every appointment lifecycle state, reducing missed service visits by 17% for field teams and end customers.
Generated 13 operational reporting endpoints for invoicing, service history, and technician utilization, shortening weekly reconciliation cycles by roughly 35% for operations managers.
Solved recurring scheduling-conflict defects by introducing database-level constraints and deterministic assignment rules, lowering duplicate booking incidents by 26%.
Improved dashboard query performance by 41% by rewriting MySQL joins, adding targeted indexes, and eliminating unnecessary data retrieval across high-traffic service-location views.
Expanded role-based permission enforcement across 19 user profiles, cutting unauthorized record-change incidents to zero throughout the final 12 months of the engagement.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
